income_thresholds.description: Covers Above Poverty Line (APL) families in Arunachal Pradesh not eligible under PM-JAY SECC criteria. Annual family income up to Rs 5 lakh.

exclusion_rules.description: Only residents of Arunachal Pradesh are eligible. Must have valid Arunachal Pradesh domicile or APST certificate.
Families covered under CGHS (Central Government Health Scheme) are excluded to avoid duplication.
Families already covered under PM-JAY SECC criteria should use PM-JAY. CMAAY is for APL families not covered by PM-JAY.

family_criteria.family_definition: Family as per ration card or APST certificate. All listed members are covered. No cap on family size.

covered_procedures: Diagnostics and lab tests during hospitalization

required_documents.name: APST Certificate / Domicile Certificate
required_documents.local_name_hi: APST प्रमाण पत्र / निवास प्रमाण पत्र
required_documents.notes: Must prove residency in Arunachal Pradesh. APST certificate or domicile certificate accepted.

required_documents.name: Income Certificate
required_documents.notes: Income certificate from Circle Officer or SDM to confirm APL status

required_documents.name: Mobile Number
required_documents.notes: Linked to Aadhaar for OTP-based verification

enrollment_steps: Step 1: Visit nearest CSC, district hospital, or block enrollment camp with Aadhaar and APST/domicile certificate
Step 2: Submit income certificate or self-declaration affidavit
Step 3: Complete e-KYC verification (Aadhaar biometric or OTP)
Step 4: Operator verifies Arunachal Pradesh residency and APL status
Step 6: Card valid immediately; present at empanelled hospital for cashless treatment up to Rs 5 lakh
description_for_embedding: Chief Minister's Arogya Arunachal Yojana (CMAAY) is Arunachal Pradesh's state health insurance scheme that supplements PM-JAY by covering Above Poverty Line (APL) families not eligible under SECC 2011 criteria. Launched in 2019, CMAAY provides Rs 5 lakh per family per year for hospitalization at empanelled hospitals. While PM-JAY covers SECC-identified families, CMAAY extends coverage to the remaining population of Arunachal Pradesh, making the state's health insurance nearly universal. Covers surgical and medical procedures across multiple specialties. Enrollment via district hospitals, CSCs, or block camps.
